characters.description: A Tier 4 Remalon and Madra's champion who arrives among the Timberlings with Niya and Mayalyn. He rejects their belief that he is a prophesied master, but intervenes when he finds a punished girl mutilated and abandoned by her sect.
characters.name: Jiran

characters.description: Jiran's cousin and traveling companion. She is with Jiran and Mayalyn as they meet the Timberlings and begin investigating the hives and the beliefs centered on Jiran.
characters.name: Niya

characters.description: Jiran's partner and a capable member of the party exploring the Timberling valley. She travels with Jiran and Niya as they investigate the hives and the punished girl's treatment.
characters.name: Mayalyn

characters.description: A young Timberling whom Jiran previously helped by saving her birther. Her sect mutilates her, sends her severed limbs as an offering, and leaves her to die after deciding that she misrepresented his earlier intervention.
characters.name: Punished Timberling Girl

characters.description: The guide of the Timberling sect of belief. She presents the prophecy centered on Jiran and reveals that her sect punished a girl for misrepresenting his earlier aid.
characters.name: Belief Sect Guide

characters.description: The restored Timberling formerly known only as the punished girl. Her sect has stripped away her old name and standing, so Jiran offers her a new name. She accepts it and becomes the party's guide among her people.
characters.name: Lulu

characters.description: An entity reached through Jiran's soul and sustained by mana he sends through a physical tap. She warns that divination has gone silent across most of the Empire and directs Jiran toward powerful beasts.
characters.name: Daughter

characters.description: An Imperial princess and diviner returning to the capital with Cameron after advancing to Tier 5. She expects to reunite with the rest of their companions.
characters.name: Olive

characters.description: Olive's cousin and a returning ally of Jiran and Mayalyn. He reaches the Imperial capital beside Olive after their recent Tier 5 advancement, expecting to reconnect with the rest of their companions.
characters.name: Cameron

characters.description: An unnamed member of the Hollow Claw clan who reveals that Stormclaw farms and kills Timberlings to support safe advancement. After Jiran heals his wings, he offers intelligence against Stormclaw and serves as a guide.
characters.name: Hollow Claw Dissenter

characters.description: Olive's father and one of the Empire's senior rulers. He knows Jiran's identity and unusual methods, wants that knowledge contained, and is alarmed when the Graymin reverse seven centuries of steady advance by retreating on every front.
characters.name: Emperor Dominus

characters.description: A Tier 3 Timberling matron who leads her people's conflict sect. She challenges Jiran and offers the sect's support if he defeats her.
characters.name: Timberling Conflict Matron

characters.description: An unidentified blonde child preserved in Sanctuary's functioning stasis chamber. An attempted revival fails because the structure lacks sufficient power, leaving the child's identity unknown and the child in stasis.
characters.name: Stasis Child

characters.description: The unidentified child in Sanctuary's stasis chamber, now identified by the chamber designation DH0001. A second revival attempt fails, and the child remains preserved until Sanctuary can obtain more power.
characters.name: DH0001

characters.description: The Timberlings' seven ancient, silent mothers. They explain how Harmony affects their people through a shared song connection and ask Jiran for permission to deliver a final prophecy.
characters.name: Seven Timberling Elders

characters.description: The progenitor who says her soul was shattered after she was brought from another world. Speaking through the seven elders, whom she identifies as preserved fragments of herself, she entrusts her descendants to Jiran and warns of a coming king. Jiran believes her fragmented soul explains the Timberlings' shared spiritual connection.
characters.name: First Timberling

characters.description: An Imperial princess who accompanies Olive and Cameron. She is discussed as a possible political match for Duran.
characters.name: Imperial Princess

characters.description: Olive's elder brother, a Tier 9 Imperial prince and northern scout. He orders troops back to Melathon and warns that the Graymin attack is imminent.
characters.name: Olive's Elder Brother

characters.description: A Timberling combat scout devoted to Jiran's cause. When her group enters an ambush by camouflaged beasts, she creates an escape for the others and remains behind, sacrificing her safety to delay the attackers.
characters.name: Knife

characters.description: A mountain-sized Tier 11 Graymin reported among the force advancing on Melathon. It is the only recorded beast to have defeated the emperors during the invasion seven centuries earlier.
characters.name: Graymin King

characters.description: A Tier 7 winged clan faction leader who seeks to unite the clans under his control. He leads the Stormclaw and Divine forces against the Rising Sky elders while pursuing Jiran as a thief.
characters.name: Stormclaw Leader

characters.description: The unnamed elder who leads the Gnashing Wound Clan and speaks for the visiting Rising Sky leaders. He identifies their agent, invites Jiran as an honored guest, and joins the elders taken to study Jiran's hunting method.
characters.name: Gnashing Wound Clan Elder

characters.description: The surviving daughter of the Stormclaw leader and twin sister of his son. Jiran finds her injured in the Land of the Lost after her father's defeat, restores her, and tests whether she took part in killing Timberlings for advancement.
characters.name: Stormclaw Leader's Daughter

characters.description: The surviving son of the Stormclaw leader and twin brother of the clan's prospective new leader. Rescued and restored alongside his sister, he supports her commitment to end the abuses committed against the Timberlings.
characters.name: Stormclaw Leader's Son

characters.description: An unusually powerful masked Tier 7 whose abilities far exceed ordinary members of his tier. He interrupts the negotiations between Jiran and the divided clans, takes control of the discussion, and demands an immense supply of weapons.
characters.name: Eldest Unspeakable

characters.description: The unnamed leader of Mayalyn's people and Jiran's former aura master. Mayalyn confronts him over his earlier betrayal, and his relationship with both her and Jiran is openly hostile.
characters.name: Mayalyn's People's Leader

characters.description: A Tier 9 member of the Imperial War Council and one of Jiran's mentors. He recognizes that the celebrated figure called Mortimer the Fireling is actually Jiran, exposing the hidden identity before the Empire's senior war leadership.
characters.name: Lenton

characters.description: The concealed identity used by Jiran, now exposed before the Imperial War Council when Lenton recognizes him. The revelation connects the reputed Fireling and his exceptional elemental ability to the young champion already known as Jiran of Feylon.
characters.name: Mortimer the Fireling

characters.description: Olive's Tier 10 formation-master mentor and an inventor of modern formation design. She agrees to help construct Melathon's defenses after Olive offers to share knowledge learned from Jiran.
characters.name: Tier 10 Formation Specialist

characters.description: An Imperial figure presented as a possible political match for the princess traveling with Olive and Cameron.
characters.name: Duran

characters.description: A former Church-linked operative who hunts assassins interfering with forces bound for the final battle. He has broken with his former allegiance, but violent intrusive voices continue to impair his judgment.
characters.name: Markhiss

characters.description: A senior Church official directing a covert operation. He approves a sacred weapon capable of destroying the Emperors and intends to obtain Jiran’s gateway by sparing allied captives who can be used to draw him out.
characters.name: Church Cardinal

characters.description: A hooded temple agent assigned to seize Jiran alive for the Cardinal while the other agents prepare to attack the Emperor.
characters.name: Three

characters.description: The commander of nine hooded temple agents watching the battle. He orders Three to capture Jiran alive for the Cardinal while directing the other agents against the emperor.
characters.name: Temple Agent Leader

characters.description: An unusually intelligent Graymin slayer that once fought Duke Reinhardt and Ravenna before escaping Jiran. It commands the specialized Tier 7 force that ambushes Jiran near the second mountain launch site.
characters.name: Graymin Slayer Commander

characters.description: A Remalon commander searching for an unidentified anomaly while her people lose cities and allied species in a failing war. She tortures a captive for the target's location and learns that the fugitive was only Tier 4 when beginning to absorb Challenger density.
characters.name: Remalon Leader

characters.description: The sentient world connected to Jiran's role as her champion and to the mana he carries.
characters.name: Madra

characters.description: Jiran's first mentor, an ascender of House Feylon who remains afflicted by an inherited curse. Although absent at the beginning of this volume, his unresolved condition continues to matter to Jiran and remains one of the obligations carried from earlier books.
characters.name: Feylon Samris
